author: arnetheduck
content: this idea is kind of flawed from the get-go  .. the constants in the spec change from time to time and we don't really coordinate those changes - there's someone ambitious implementing this kind of check coming here every time there's a new constant .. I feel like the solution here is to note in the spec that consumers must simply be ready to ignore unknown constants and should only expect the constants that they actually need for their application to be present - this way, consumers don't have to do anything if a hard-fork doesn't touch actually relevant constants

author: tersec
content: a "timeline" is a request to keep

author: tersec
content: either it's in the specs or not
